scope.summary: Let the desktop app log in / listen by having the Rust core read .zalo-cred.json from disk, so the bearer token never enters the webview. Replaces the paste-JSON box with file-backed buttons for GUI testing.
goals: command/ exposes cred_file_summary, login_from_file, start_listening_from_file reading a fixed repo-local session path
The credential path is fixed (env ZALO_CRED_FILE or repo-root .zalo-cred.json); the UI cannot supply an arbitrary path
Frontend uses buttons (Check session / Log in / Log in + listen) with no credential textarea
nonGoals: Final credential UX (file picker + OS keychain) — that is secure-cred-store

acceptance.description: The dev cred path is fixed and repo-local; a missing file returns a clear value-free error.
Frontend builds with the file-backed buttons UI (no credential textarea).
Live: the core reads .zalo-cred.json and logs in (account id present); GUI login verified via tauri dev.
